summary: Large decompilation PR for Link's hookshot item module, adding nearly the full `src/melee/it/items/itlinkhookshot.c` implementation and supporting item/fighter/collision type definitions. The PR title reports 74% match for `itlinkhookshot`. The diff shows new hookshot state-table setup, spawn/despawn handling, chain segment GObj allocation, matrix/vector update helpers, collision setup through `mpColl`, fighter motion checks for Link/Young Link hookshot actions, and item variable/attribute structs needed to express the module in C. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided slice, so intent and reviewer guidance are inferred only from changed files and diff content.

key_files.change: Added approximately 1994 lines implementing the Link hookshot item module, including state table `it_803F6998`, global `it_804D6D48`, item callbacks, chain segment setup, matrix/vector helpers, collision updates, and interactions with fighter state.
Expanded hookshot header declarations; exact declarations are not fully visible in the provided excerpt.
Added `ItemLink`, `itLinkHookshotVars`, and `itLinkHookshotAttributes` structs, plus an `lb/types.h` include for shared types.
Removed item hookshot-related placeholder content from the broader item types header, likely moving it into `itCharItems.h`; exact deleted definitions are not fully visible in the excerpt.
Refined several `ftLk_DatAttrs` fields from `UNK_T` or generic `int` to concrete `s32`, `float`, or `int` types at offsets `0x88`, `0x98`, `0xA8`, `0xB4`, and `0xB8`.
Small collision header adjustment supporting hookshot collision usage; exact symbol change is not shown in the excerpt.

what_changed: Implemented a large portion of `itlinkhookshot.c`, transforming the file from effectively empty to a 1995-line item module.

Added `ItemStateTable it_803F6998[]` with nine hookshot item states wired to callbacks such as `it_802A2ED0`, `it_802A2D88`, `it_802A30FC`, `it_802A3240`, `it_802A338C`, `it_802A34EC`, `it_802A361C`, `it_802A3814`, `it_802A39E8`, and `it_802A3C84`.

Added hookshot item lifecycle helpers including clearing callback storage in `item->xDD4_itemVar.linkhookshot.x10`, resizing based on owner/item scale, freeing `ItemLink` user data, and deleting chain GObjs.
Modeled the hookshot chain as linked `ItemLink` GObjs with `prev`/`next`, velocity, current/previous position, bitflags, embedded `CollData`, and associated GObj/JObj pointers.

Added hookshot spawn logic in `it_802A2BA4`, including `SpawnItem` initialization, fighter motion gating through `link_fighter_compare`, chain creation with `it_802A2568`, parent attachment with `Item_8026AB54`, and assignment of the hookshot JObj into fighter parts.
Added attribute-driven chain math via `itLinkHookshotAttributes`, including helper math that scales derived fields by fighter scale and computes a segment count-like value stored in `attr->x2C`.
Added static inline helpers for repeated JObj loading, matrix setup, matrix-to-Vec3 extraction, linear interpolation, and chain segment initialization.
Added collision initialization for each hookshot chain link using `mpColl_80041EE4`, `mpColl_8004220C`, and `CollData` topn fields.

Added item/fighter interaction paths using fighter callbacks and common fighter routines such as `ftCo_800C3CC0`, `ftCommon_8007E2F4`, cliff/damage/catch-related includes, and Link/Young Link motion checks.
Added effects/audio-adjacent integration points visible through includes and calls such as `efSync_Spawn` for hookshot impact/visual effects.
Added concrete type information to Link data attributes needed by the hookshot work, replacing several unknowns with `s32`, `float`, or `int`.
smart_moves: Introduced `ItemLink` as the explicit user-data struct for hookshot chain segment GObjs instead of keeping the chain as opaque offsets, making linked-list traversal, collision data, and JObj/GObj ownership legible.

Separated hookshot runtime variables (`itLinkHookshotVars`) from data-driven attributes (`itLinkHookshotAttributes`), matching the common item pattern of per-item state in `xDD4_itemVar` and article attributes in `xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes`.
Used small static inline helpers for repeated matrix/JObj/vector sequences, which is a common matching tactic when the compiler inlines repeated low-level math while preserving readable C structure.
Kept several helpers address-named, such as `it_802A2568`, `it_802A2BA4`, and `it_802A2EE4`, avoiding premature semantic names where behavior was only partially understood.
Gated spawn behavior through explicit fighter motion IDs in `link_fighter_compare`, localizing the still-unnamed motion-state knowledge instead of scattering those constants throughout the module.
Cleaned up allocated chain GObjs on failure during chain creation, showing attention to original control flow and resource lifetime rather than just the successful path.
Refined `ftLk_DatAttrs` field types only where required by usage, preserving unknowns elsewhere and limiting speculative header churn.
decomp_lessons: For item modules with dynamically created sub-objects, define the sub-object user-data struct early; it can unlock cleaner matching for linked lists, collision updates, and render callbacks.
Hookshot-style chain objects may require modeling both item state and multiple child GObjs/JObjs; expect interactions across item, fighter parts, baselib GObj/JObj, and collision systems.

When matching matrix-heavy code, small inline helpers around `PSMTXIdentity`, `HSD_JObjSetupMatrix`, `PSMTXConcat`, and Vec3 extraction can help reproduce compiler output while documenting the repeated operation.
Do not over-name behavior just because many functions are now decompiled; address-based names are acceptable until semantics are stable.
Struct field typing can be driven by decompiled call sites: `s32` for motion/count-like values, `f32` for scalar attributes, and `HSD_Joint*` for loaded joint data in article attributes.
For item articles tied to fighter body parts, track both the owning fighter GObj and the attached JObj, and verify cleanup resets fighter callbacks/fields that reference the item.
Collision structs embedded in custom item link data need their current and previous topn positions initialized before calling mp collision setup routines.
Preserve evidence of compiler-sensitive alternatives, such as inline helper variants, when they explain why a particular C shape was chosen for matching.

naming_conventions.lesson: Use address names for newly matched functions when semantic names are not yet proven.
Runtime vars and article attributes follow the item/module prefix style and distinguish mutable state from data attributes.
Keep offset-derived names where exact semantic meaning is uncertain, while adding type information and nested struct names where known.
Small static helper names can document repeated operations without renaming externally visible address functions.

naming_conventions.pattern: Flag fields in unknown bitfield structs
naming_conventions.lesson: For bitflags with unclear meaning, neutral numeric names are safer than guessed behavior names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `static inline` helper functions to shape repeated codegen, including chain initialization, JObj loading, attribute math, matrix setup, and Vec3 extraction.
Used explicit casts and concrete types around GObj/item access, such as `GET_ITEM((HSD_GObj*) obj)` and `GET_FIGHTER(item->owner)`, to match existing macro expectations and compiler behavior.

Kept exact float constants visible, including `1.875F`, `6.2831855f`, `0.6499999761581421`, and `0.3499999940395355`, rather than replacing them with higher-level constants that might perturb matching.
Used local padding arrays such as `f32 pad[8]`, `f32 pad[2]`, and `f32 pad[1]` in visible functions, indicating stack-layout-sensitive matching.
Preserved motion IDs as numeric constants in `link_fighter_compare` and spawn logic, likely because named enum coverage was not yet available or would affect confidence.
Structured allocation failure cleanup to mirror original control flow: if `GObj_Create` fails during chain creation, previously allocated chain GObjs are unlinked/deleted before returning `NULL`.
Loaded alternating hookshot chain joints based on segment index parity via `it_link_get_joint`, and loaded a distinct terminal joint via `it_link_get_joint_c`, matching the article attribute layout.
